title: Pilosella dilutior
taxonomy: Pilosella dilutior belongs to the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. It is a species of flowering plant in the genus Pilosella, which is part of the larger subfamily Cichorioideae. This plant is closely related to other species within the genus, such as Pilosella officinarum and Pilosella epirota. The genus Pilosella is further divided into several sections, with Pilosella dilutior being part of the section Pilosella. The classification of Pilosella dilutior reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants within the Asteraceae family.
uses: Pilosella dilutior has been reported to have various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. It is used in traditional medicine to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues. The plant's leaves and stems are used to prepare infusions and decoctions, which are believed to have therapeutic benefits. However, further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Pilosella dilutior for medicinal purposes. Additionally, the plant's properties may vary depending on the region and specific preparation methods used.

preparations: Pilosella dilutior is a plant species that has been used in traditional herbal preparations. side_effects: Pilosella dilutior, while not commonly associated with significant side effects, may cause skin irritation or allergic reactions in some individuals due to its sap. Its sap can also cause phytophotodermatitis, a condition characterized by skin blisters or rashes upon exposure to sunlight. Ingestion of Pilosella dilutior is not recommended as it may cause gastrointestinal upset. There is limited information available on the long-term effects of exposure to Pilosella dilutior, and further research is needed to fully understand its potential risks. Individuals handling the plant should exercise caution and wear protective clothing to minimize potential side effects.
intro: Pilosella dilutior, also known as Dillenii's mouse-ear hawkweed, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion tribe within the sunflower family, known for its small yellow daisy-like flowers. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it a possible natural remedy for various conditions such as arthritis and skin issues. The bioactive constituents of Pilosella dilutior include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditional herbal preparations made from Pilosella dilutior include infusions, tinctures, and dried herb extracts, often used to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome.
